#aggregate_llm_obs_experimentation(body, opts = {}) ⇒ Object

Aggregate LLM Observability experimentation.

# File 'lib/datadog_api_client/v2/api/llm_observability_api.rb', line 29

def aggregate_llm_obs_experimentation(body, opts = {})
  data, _status_code, _headers = aggregate_llm_obs_experimentation_with_http_info(body, opts)
  data
end

#aggregate_llm_obs_experimentation_with_http_info(body, opts = {}) ⇒ Array<(LLMObsExperimentationAnalyticsResponse, Integer, Hash)>

Aggregate LLM Observability experimentation.

Execute an analytics aggregation over LLM Observability experimentation data. Use this endpoint to compute metrics (for example average eval scores) grouped by fields such as span_id or experiment_id.

At least one compute definition and one index must be provided.

Parameters:

  • body (LLMObsExperimentationAnalyticsRequest) —

    Analytics payload.

  • opts (Hash) (defaults to: {}) —

    the optional parameters

Returns:

  • (Array<(LLMObsExperimentationAnalyticsResponse, Integer, Hash)>) —

    LLMObsExperimentationAnalyticsResponse data, response status code and response headers

#batch_update_llm_obs_dataset_with_http_info(project_id, dataset_id, body, opts = {}) ⇒ Array<(LLMObsDatasetRecordsMutationResponse, Integer, Hash)>

Batch update LLM Observability dataset records.

Insert, update, and delete records in a single dataset operation. By default, a new dataset version is created when the batch is applied.

Parameters:

  • project_id (String) —

    The ID of the LLM Observability project.

  • dataset_id (String) —

    The ID of the LLM Observability dataset.

  • body (LLMObsDatasetBatchUpdateRequest) —

    Batch update payload.

  • opts (Hash) (defaults to: {}) —

    the optional parameters

Clone an LLM Observability dataset.

Clone a dataset, copying its current records into a new dataset within the same project.

Parameters:

    The ID of the LLM Observability project.

  • dataset_id (String) —

    The ID of the source LLM Observability dataset to clone.

  • body (LLMObsDatasetCloneRequest) —

    Clone dataset payload.

  • opts (Hash) (defaults to: {}) —

    the optional parameters

Returns:

  • (Array<(LLMObsDatasetResponse, Integer, Hash)>) —

    LLMObsDatasetResponse data, response status code and response headers

def create_llm_obs_annotation_queue_interactions(queue_id, body, opts = {})

  data
end

#create_llm_obs_annotation_queue_interactions_with_http_info(queue_id, body, opts = {}) ⇒ Array<(LLMObsAnnotationQueueInteractionsResponse, Integer, Hash)>

Add annotation queue interactions.

Add one or more interactions to an annotation queue. At least one interaction must be provided. Each interaction has a type:

  • trace, experiment_trace, session: content_id references the upstream entity; the server fetches the actual content.
  • display_block: omit content_id and provide the rendered content in display_block. The server generates content_id as a deterministic hash of the block list.

Items of different types can be mixed in a single request.

Parameters:

  • queue_id (String) —

    The ID of the LLM Observability annotation queue.

  • body (LLMObsAnnotationQueueInteractionsRequest) —

    Add interactions payload.

  • opts (Hash) (defaults to: {}) —

    the optional parameters

Returns:

  • (Array<(LLMObsAnnotationQueueInteractionsResponse, Integer, Hash)>) —

    LLMObsAnnotationQueueInteractionsResponse data, response status code and response headers

#create_llm_obs_annotation_queue_with_http_info(body, opts = {}) ⇒ Array<(LLMObsAnnotationQueueResponse, Integer, Hash)>

Create an LLM Observability annotation queue.

Create an annotation queue. The name and project_id fields are required. An optional annotation_schema can be provided to define the labels for the queue. Fields such as created_by, owned_by, created_at, modified_by, and modified_at are inferred by the backend.

Parameters:

  • body (LLMObsAnnotationQueueRequest) —

    Create annotation queue payload.

  • opts (Hash) (defaults to: {}) —

    the optional parameters

Returns:

  • (Array<(LLMObsAnnotationQueueResponse, Integer, Hash)>) —

    LLMObsAnnotationQueueResponse data, response status code and response headers

#export_llm_obs_dataset_with_http_info(project_id, dataset_id, opts = {}) ⇒ Array<(String, Integer, Hash)>

Export an LLM Observability dataset.

Download the contents of a dataset as a CSV file. The download is streamed and includes one row per dataset record.

Parameters:

  • project_id (String) —

    The ID of the LLM Observability project.

  • dataset_id (String) —

    The ID of the LLM Observability dataset.

  • opts (Hash) (defaults to: {}) —

    the optional parameters

Options Hash (opts):

  • :format (LLMObsDatasetExportFormat) —

    Export format for the dataset contents. Only csv is currently supported.

  • :version (Integer) —

    Version of the dataset to export. If omitted, the current version is used. Must be between 0 and the current version of the dataset, inclusive.

Returns:

  • (Array<(String, Integer, Hash)>) —

    String data, response status code and response headers

#get_llm_obs_annotated_interactions_by_trace_i_ds_with_http_info(content_ids, opts = {}) ⇒ Array<(LLMObsAnnotatedInteractionsByTraceResponse, Integer, Hash)>

Get annotated interactions by content IDs.

Returns annotated interactions across all annotation queues for the given content IDs. Results include queue metadata (ID and name) for each interaction.

Parameters:

  • content_ids (Array<String>) —

    One or more content IDs to retrieve annotated interactions for. At least one is required.

  • opts (Hash) (defaults to: {}) —

    the optional parameters

Options Hash (opts):

  • :offset (Integer) —

    Pagination offset. Must be >= 0. Defaults to 0.

  • :limit (Integer) —

    Maximum number of results to return. Must be > 0. Defaults to 100.

Returns:

  • (Array<(LLMObsAnnotatedInteractionsByTraceResponse, Integer, Hash)>) —

    LLMObsAnnotatedInteractionsByTraceResponse data, response status code and response headers

#get_llm_obs_prompt_with_http_info(prompt_id, opts = {}) ⇒ Array<(LLMObsPromptSDKResponse, Integer, Hash)>

Get an LLM Observability prompt.

Get the latest version of an LLM Observability prompt by prompt ID.

Parameters:

  • prompt_id (String) —

    The customer-provided identifier of the LLM Observability prompt.

  • opts (Hash) (defaults to: {}) —

    the optional parameters

Options Hash (opts):

  • :label (String) —

    Deprecated. Optional label of the prompt version to return. Do not use this parameter for new integrations. If omitted, the latest version is returned. If the prompt has no labels, the latest version is returned even when a label is requested. If the prompt has labels but none match the requested label, a 404 response is returned.

Returns:

  • (Array<(LLMObsPromptSDKResponse, Integer, Hash)>) —

    LLMObsPromptSDKResponse data, response status code and response headers

#list_llm_obs_annotation_queues_with_http_info(opts = {}) ⇒ Array<(LLMObsAnnotationQueuesResponse, Integer, Hash)>

List LLM Observability annotation queues.

List annotation queues. Optionally filter by project ID or queue IDs. These parameters are mutually exclusive. If neither is provided, all queues in the organization are returned.

Parameters:

  • opts (Hash) (defaults to: {}) —

    the optional parameters

Options Hash (opts):

  • :project_id (String) —

    Filter annotation queues by project ID. Cannot be used together with queueIds.

  • :queue_ids (Array<String>) —

    Filter annotation queues by queue IDs (comma-separated). Cannot be used together with projectId.

Returns:

  • (Array<(LLMObsAnnotationQueuesResponse, Integer, Hash)>) —

    LLMObsAnnotationQueuesResponse data, response status code and response headers

List LLM Observability dataset records.

List all records in an LLM Observability dataset, sorted by creation date, newest first.

Parameters:

    The ID of the LLM Observability project.

  • dataset_id (String) —

    The ID of the LLM Observability dataset.

    the optional parameters

Options Hash (opts):

    Use the Pagination cursor to retrieve the next page of results.

  • :page_limit (Integer) —

    Maximum number of results to return per page.

#list_llm_obs_experiments_with_http_info(opts = {}) ⇒ Array<(LLMObsExperimentsResponse, Integer, Hash)>

List LLM Observability experiments.

List all LLM Observability experiments sorted by creation date, newest first.

Parameters:

  • opts (Hash) (defaults to: {}) —

    the optional parameters

Options Hash (opts):

  • :filter_project_id (String) —

    Filter experiments by project ID. Required if filter[dataset_id] is not provided.

  • :filter_dataset_id (String) —

    Filter experiments by dataset ID.

  • :filter_id (String) —

    Filter experiments by experiment ID. Can be specified multiple times.

  • :filter_name (String) —

    Filter experiments by their exact run name.

  • :filter_experiment (String) —

    Filter by logical experiment name. This is the name field set when creating an experiment through POST /experiments. Returns all experiment runs that share the same name, enabling cross-commit and cross-branch comparisons.

  • :filter_metadata (String) —

    Filter by JSONB metadata containment. Provide a JSON object string where experiments whose metadata contains all specified key-value pairs are returned. For example: {"commit":"abc123","branch":"main"}.

  • :filter_parent_experiment_id (String) —

    Filter experiments by the ID of their parent (baseline) experiment. Returns all experiments that were run against the given baseline. Can be specified multiple times.

  • :filter_is_deleted (Boolean) —

    When true, return only soft-deleted experiments. Defaults to false.

  • :include_user_data (Boolean) —

    When true, enrich each experiment with its author's user data in the author field.

  • :include_dataset_names (Boolean) —

    When true, enrich each experiment with its dataset name in the dataset_name field.

  • :page_cursor (String) —

    Use the pagination cursor returned in meta.after to retrieve the next page of results.

  • :page_limit (Integer) —

    Maximum number of results to return per page. Values above 5000 are clamped to 5000. Defaults to 5000.

Returns:

  • (Array<(LLMObsExperimentsResponse, Integer, Hash)>) —

    LLMObsExperimentsResponse data, response status code and response headers

List patterns clustered points.

List the data points grouped into a topic. For a parent topic, points from all of its leaf topics are returned.

Parameters:

  • topic_id (String) —

    The ID of the topic to retrieve clustered points for.

  • opts (Hash) (defaults to: {}) —

    the optional parameters

Options Hash (opts):

  • :page_size (Integer) —

    Maximum number of clustered points to return per page.

  • :page_token (String) —

    Pagination token to retrieve the next page of clustered points.

Returns:

  • (Array<(LLMObsPatternsClusteredPointsResponse, Integer, Hash)>) —

    LLMObsPatternsClusteredPointsResponse data, response status code and response headers

#list_llm_obs_patterns_topics_with_clustered_points_with_http_info(config_id, opts = {}) ⇒ Array<(LLMObsPatternsTopicsWithClusteredPointsResponse, Integer, Hash)>

List patterns topics with clustered points.

List the topics discovered by a patterns run, with the clustered points attached inline to each leaf topic. When no run is specified, the most recent completed run is used.

Parameters:

  • config_id (String) —

    The ID of the patterns configuration.

  • opts (Hash) (defaults to: {}) —

    the optional parameters

Options Hash (opts):

  • :run_id (String) —

    The ID of a specific patterns run. Defaults to the most recent completed run.

  • :include_metrics (Boolean) —

    When true, enrich each clustered point with span metrics such as status, duration, token counts, estimated cost, and evaluations.

Returns:

  • (Array<(LLMObsPatternsTopicsWithClusteredPointsResponse, Integer, Hash)>) —

    LLMObsPatternsTopicsWithClusteredPointsResponse data, response status code and response headers

#list_llm_obs_spans_with_http_info(opts = {}) ⇒ Array<(LLMObsSpansResponse, Integer, Hash)>

List LLM Observability spans.

List LLM Observability spans matching the specified filters.

Parameters:

  • opts (Hash) (defaults to: {}) —

    the optional parameters

Options Hash (opts):

  • :filter_from (String) —

    Start of the time range. Accepts ISO 8601 or relative format (e.g., now-15m). Defaults to now-15m.

  • :filter_to (String) —

    End of the time range. Accepts ISO 8601 or relative format. Defaults to now.

  • :filter_query (String) —

    Search query using LLM Observability query syntax. Supports attribute filters using the field:value syntax (e.g. session_id, trace_id, ml_app, meta.span.kind). When provided, structured field filters (filter[span_id], filter[trace_id], etc.) are ignored.

  • :filter_span_id (String) —

    Filter by exact span ID.

  • :filter_trace_id (String) —

    Filter by exact trace ID.

  • :filter_span_kind (String) —

    Filter by span kind (e.g., llm, agent, tool, task, workflow).

  • :filter_span_name (String) —

    Filter by span name.

  • :filter_ml_app (String) —

    Filter by ML application name.

  • :page_limit (Integer) —

    Maximum number of spans to return. Defaults to 10.

  • :page_cursor (String) —

    Cursor from the previous response to retrieve the next page.

  • :sort (String) —

    Sort order for the results.

  • :include_attachments (Boolean) —

    Whether to include attachment data in the response. Defaults to true.

Returns:

  • (Array<(LLMObsSpansResponse, Integer, Hash)>) —

    LLMObsSpansResponse data, response status code and response headers

Lock LLM Observability dataset draft state.

Acquire the draft lock on a dataset for the calling user. The lock prevents other users from concurrently editing the dataset draft.

    The ID of the LLM Observability project.

#restore_llm_obs_dataset_version_with_http_info(project_id, dataset_id, body, opts = {}) ⇒ Array<(nil, Integer, Hash)>

Restore an LLM Observability dataset version.

Restore a dataset to a previous version. The dataset's current version is bumped, and its records are replaced with the records from the specified prior version.

Parameters:

  • project_id (String) —

    The ID of the LLM Observability project.

  • dataset_id (String) —

    The ID of the LLM Observability dataset.

  • body (LLMObsDatasetRestoreVersionRequest) —

    Restore dataset version payload.

  • opts (Hash) (defaults to: {}) —

    the optional parameters

Returns:

  • (Array<(nil, Integer, Hash)>) —

    nil, response status code and response headers

#search_llm_obs_experimentation_with_http_info(body, opts = {}) ⇒ Array<(LLMObsExperimentationSearchResponse, Integer, Hash)>

Search LLM Observability experimentation entities.

Search across LLM Observability experimentation entities — projects, datasets, dataset records, experiments, and experiment runs — using cursor-based pagination.

The filter.scope field controls which entity types are returned. At least one valid scope must be provided.

Returns 200 OK when all results fit in a single page. Returns 206 Partial Content with a cursor in meta.after when additional pages are available.

Parameters:

  • body (LLMObsExperimentationSearchRequest) —

    Experimentation search payload.

  • opts (Hash) (defaults to: {}) —

    the optional parameters

Returns:

  • (Array<(LLMObsExperimentationSearchResponse, Integer, Hash)>) —

    LLMObsExperimentationSearchResponse data, response status code and response headers

#update_llm_obs_annotation_queue_label_schema_with_http_info(queue_id, body, opts = {}) ⇒ Array<(LLMObsAnnotationQueueLabelSchemaResponse, Integer, Hash)>

Update annotation queue label schema.

Create or replace the label schema for a given annotation queue. The label schema defines the labels annotators can apply to interactions in the queue. Label names must be unique within the queue and match the pattern ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$. Each label must have a valid type: score, categorical, boolean, or text.

Parameters:

  • queue_id (String) —

    The ID of the LLM Observability annotation queue.

  • body (LLMObsAnnotationQueueLabelSchemaUpdateRequest) —

    Update label schema payload.

  • opts (Hash) (defaults to: {}) —

    the optional parameters

Returns:

  • (Array<(LLMObsAnnotationQueueLabelSchemaResponse, Integer, Hash)>) —

    LLMObsAnnotationQueueLabelSchemaResponse data, response status code and response headers

#upload_llm_obs_dataset_records_file(project_id, dataset_id, opts = {}) ⇒ Object

Upload records to an LLM Observability dataset.

See Also:

  • #upload_llm_obs_dataset_records_file_with_http_info


5345

# File 'lib/datadog_api_client/v2/api/llm_observability_api.rb', line 5345

def upload_llm_obs_dataset_records_file(project_id, dataset_id, opts = {})
  upload_llm_obs_dataset_records_file_with_http_info(project_id, dataset_id, opts)
  nil
end

#upload_llm_obs_dataset_records_file_with_http_info(project_id, dataset_id, opts = {}) ⇒ Array<(nil, Integer, Hash)>

Upload records to an LLM Observability dataset.

Upload records to a dataset from a file. The request is a multipart/form-data upload containing a single file part. Currently only CSV is supported. The CSV must include an input column. Optional columns are id, expected_output, metadata, and tags.

The response is a Server-Sent Events stream (text/event-stream) emitting progress updates while records are processed. The stream emits the following named events:

  • progress: incremental record counts written so far.
  • completed: terminal event with a JSON body containing records_created.
  • error: terminal event with a JSON body containing an error message.

Parameters:

  • project_id (String) —

    The ID of the LLM Observability project.

  • dataset_id (String) —

    The ID of the LLM Observability dataset.

  • opts (Hash) (defaults to: {}) —

    the optional parameters

Options Hash (opts):

  • :deduplicate (Boolean) —

    Whether to skip records whose input already exists in the dataset. Defaults to false.

  • :overwrite (Boolean) —

    Whether to overwrite existing records that share the same user-provided id. Defaults to true.

  • :tags (Array<String>) —

    Tags to apply to every uploaded record, in addition to any tags defined on individual rows. Can be repeated, e.g. tags=env:prod&tags=team:ai.

  • :include_user_data (Boolean) —

    Whether to enrich the response with user metadata.

  • :file (File) —

    The records file to upload. Currently only CSV is supported. The file must include an input column. Optional columns include id, expected_output, metadata, and tags.

Returns:

  • (Array<(nil, Integer, Hash)>) —

    nil, response status code and response headers

#upsert_llm_obs_annotations_with_http_info(queue_id, body, opts = {}) ⇒ Array<(LLMObsAnnotationsResponse, Integer, Hash)>

Create or update annotations.

Create or update annotations on interactions in a queue. Each annotation is matched by interaction_id and the requesting user's identity. Results and errors in the response are linked to request items by interaction_id. Errors for individual items are returned in the errors field without blocking the rest of the batch.

Parameters:

  • queue_id (String) —

    The ID of the LLM Observability annotation queue.

  • body (LLMObsAnnotationsRequest) —

    Payload for creating or updating annotations.

  • opts (Hash) (defaults to: {}) —

    the optional parameters

Returns:

  • (Array<(LLMObsAnnotationsResponse, Integer, Hash)>) —

    LLMObsAnnotationsResponse data, response status code and response headers
